text:
Once upon a time, in a small town bustling with vibrant life, there lived a bookworm named Eliana. She had ears that could absorb stories like a vacuum. Every evening, she settled into her favorite armchair, her nose crinkling with delight as she dipped into classical novels.

There was a peculiar character named Henri whom Eliana met at the library. Henri had a knack for solving mysteries... and he had a pet owl named Whiskers. One rainy afternoon, Henri invited Eliana to his home, hoping his wisdom could solve a tangled web of events he'd stumbled across.

Eliana listened intently as Henri regaled her with tales of mysterious locks of hair found near ghost towns. Somehow these locks opened the doors to forgotten treasures.

The next day, Eliana discovered a lock on her own door. When she squeezed it, a tiny flute fell out. Curious, she played it—young Mozart seemed to live inside. She visited Henri again.

Over tea,
